title: Introduction to GitHub Copilot
objectives: Describe GitHub Copilot capabilities and features
Setup and configure Copilot in your IDE

sections.title: GitHub Copilot — Your AI Pair Programmer
sections.body:
AI is transforming software development by helping teams build, test, and deliver applications more efficiently. GitHub Copilot acts as an AI pair programmer, supporting developers across popular programming languages.

Key reported benefits include:
- 46% of new code generated by AI
- 55% faster developer productivity
- 74% of developers feeling more focused on meaningful work

GitHub Copilot was developed by GitHub and Microsoft in collaboration with OpenAI. It is powered by OpenAI Codex, a model specialized in code generation and trained on large amounts of public source code.
bullets: 46% of new code generated by AI

sections.title: GitHub Copilot Features
sections.body:
GitHub Copilot provides multiple capabilities across development environments:

**Copilot Chat** — Interactive chat directly in supported editors to ask questions, get explanations, generate tests, and explore new features.

**Pull Request Summaries** — Automatically generate summaries of PR changes and impact analysis.

**Code Review Assistance** — AI-assisted suggestions during code review including potential bugs and security issues.

**Copilot for the CLI** — Use Copilot in the command line to explain shell commands and learn scripting patterns.

**Copilot Spaces** — Dedicated chat contexts grounded in curated repositories and documentation.

**Copilot Cloud Agent** — Advanced agent-based workflows for complex, multi-step development tasks.

sections.title: Interaction Modes — All Methods
sections.body:
**Inline Suggestions** — Real-time code completions as you type, predicting what you want to write next.

**Command Palette** — Quick access to Copilot functions with keyboard shortcuts.

**Copilot Chat** — Interactive natural language communication for complex requests.

**Inline Chat** — Context-specific conversations directly in your code editor using Ctrl+I (Windows/Linux) or Cmd+I (Mac).

**Slash Commands** — Shortcuts like /explain, /suggest, /tests, /comment for common tasks.

**Comments to Code** — Describe functionality in natural language comments; Copilot generates the implementation.

**Automated Test Generation** — Select code and use command palette to generate unit tests.

sections.title: Setup and Configuration
sections.body:
Setting up Copilot requires:
- Install the Copilot extension in VS Code, Visual Studio, or supported editor
- Install GitHub Copilot CLI for command-line assistance
- Authenticate with GitHub account
- Ensure network access and proper permissions

Configuration considerations include editor integration settings, language/framework support, privacy preferences, and organizational policies.

sections.title: Practice with Copilot: Student and Class Onboarding
sections.body:
### Real-World Use Case
Business Context:
A school is launching a web portal for teachers. Teachers need to quickly register students and assign them to classes without manual spreadsheets.

Developer Task:
Use Copilot interaction modes to build POST /students and POST /classes endpoints in Express with MongoDB validation and error handling.

Example Input:
```json
{
  "student": {
    "studentId": "STD-3007",
    "fullName": "Sara Idrissi",
    "email": "sara.idrissi@school.edu"
  },
  "class": {
    "classCode": "MATH-8A",
    "teacher": "Mr. Karim",
    "room": "B12"
  }
}
```

Expected Output:
- Express routes for student and class creation.
- MongoDB persistence with duplicate checks.
- 201 for success and 400/409 for invalid or duplicate data.

Why This Matters:
Accurate onboarding reduces admin workload and prevents scheduling errors.

GitHub Copilot Challenge:
Ask Copilot to scaffold routes and models, then improve validation, duplicate checks, and integration tests.

exercise.title: Implement student and class onboarding APIs with Copilot
steps: Scaffold Student and Class schemas with inline suggestions.
Generate POST /students and POST /classes handlers using inline chat.
Add duplicate handling and structured errors with Copilot Chat.
Generate tests for success, invalid payload, and duplicates.
Refine code readability and docs before final review.
expectedOutcomes: Better use of Copilot interaction modes.
troubleshooting: If validation is missing, ask Copilot for schema-level validators.
If responses are inconsistent, define a shared error format.
If tests are unstable, isolate test data and reset DB state.

steps.title: Setup — clone, install, first run
steps.copilotHint: Verify the app starts with npm test.

steps.title: Scaffold Student and Class validation
steps.copilotHint: Use inline chat to implement validators.js.

steps.title: Implement POST /students
steps.copilotHint: Use inline chat for the students route.

steps.title: Implement POST /classes
steps.copilotHint: Use Copilot Chat for the classes route.

steps.title: Final integration and LAB-NOTES.md
steps.copilotHint: Complete integration tests and document your Copilot workflow.

quiz.question: What is GitHub Copilot?
options: GitHub Copilot is an AI pair programmer that you can use to get code suggestions.
GitHub Copilot is OpenAI Codex, a new AI system that OpenAI created.
GitHub Copilot is a JavaScript public repository and is one of the best-supported languages.
GitHub Copilot can write a comment that describes logic, and you can add your suggested code.

options: GitHub Copilot Enterprise has code completions, whereas GitHub Copilot Business doesn't.
GitHub Copilot Enterprise has chat in IDE and mobile, whereas GitHub Copilot Business doesn't.
GitHub Copilot Enterprise has an extra layer of personalization using organization's codebase.
GitHub Copilot Enterprise has IP indemnity and enterprise-grade security, safety, and privacy.
